videos.title: Warsaw Poland Travel Guide: 14 BEST Things to Do in Warsaw
videos.description:
How to find cheap flights and travel more: https://travelforalmostfree.com/ref=beforeyougo2 // This Warsaw travel guide shares the best things to do in Warsaw.

-----------------
Recommended Tours in Warsaw:

Private Tour: Warsaw City Sightseeing by Retro Fiat: https://www.viator.com/tours/Warsaw/Private-Tour-Warsaw-City-Sightseeing-by-Retro-Fiat/d528-6875MUSTSEE?pid=P00104625&mcid=42383&medium=link&medium_version=selector&campaign=yt-warsaw-desc

Polish Dumpling Cooking Class, Lunch or Dinner with Liqueur Tasting: https://www.viator.com/tours/Warsaw/PIEROGI-and-MORE-cooking-class/d528-118457P1?pid=P00104625&mcid=42383&medium=link&medium_version=selector&campaign=yt-warsaw-desc

Everyday concerts of Chopin's music at the Old Town in Warsaw: https://www.viator.com/tours/Warsaw/Everyday-concerts-of-Chopins-music-at-the-Old-Town-in-Warsaw/d528-64757P1?pid=P00104625&mcid=42383&medium=link&medium_version=selector&campaign=yt-warsaw-desc

Krakow and Auschwitz Small Group Tour from Warsaw with Lunch: https://www.viator.com/tours/Warsaw/Krakow-and-Auschwitz-1-day-tour-from-Warsaw/d528-26942P1?pid=P00104625&mcid=42383&medium=link&medium_version=selector&campaign=yt-warsaw-desc
-----------------

Welcome to the vibrant city of Warsaw, Poland! In this immersive video, we invite you to discover the top things to do and see in this captivating capital.

Explore the historic Old Town of Warsaw, a UNESCO World Heritage site meticulously reconstructed after World War II. Wander through cobblestone streets, admire colorful facades, and visit the Royal Castle to immerse yourself in the city's rich history.

Experience the poignant history of the Warsaw Uprising at the Warsaw Uprising Museum. Learn about the heroic efforts of the Polish resistance during WWII through immersive exhibits, artifacts, and personal stories.

Indulge in Warsaw's culinary scene at local cafes, restaurants, and traditional milk bars. Sample Polish specialties like pierogi (dumplings), kielbasa (sausage), and traditional pastries.

Visit the awe-inspiring Palace of Culture and Science, an iconic skyscraper that dominates the city's skyline. Take an elevator to the observation deck for panoramic views of Warsaw's urban landscape.

Explore the vibrant Praga district, known for its artistic spirit and bohemian vibe. Discover street art, vintage shops, and unique galleries, and experience the area's revitalized cultural scene.

Immerse yourself in the history of the POLIN Museum of the History of Polish Jews. Learn about the rich and diverse Jewish heritage of Poland through interactive exhibits, historical artifacts, and engaging narratives.

Experience the serene beauty of Lazienki Park, a vast green oasis in the heart of the city. Visit the grand Lazienki Palace, stroll along picturesque pathways, and relax by the tranquil lake.

Discover the Warsaw Rising Monument, a powerful tribute to the heroes of the 1944 Warsaw Uprising. Learn about the resilience of the Polish people and the city's tumultuous history.

Join us as we uncover the hidden gems and must-visit attractions of Warsaw, a city that seamlessly blends history, culture, and modernity.

Get ready to be captivated by the allure of Warsaw in this immersive video experience. So, pack your bags, and let the adventure begin in this remarkable city where history, culture, and warm Polish hospitality await!

Keep watching to see the top things to do in Warsaw, top attractions in Warsaw, places to visit in Warsaw and Poland travel guides.

Subscribe for travel inspiration videos and travel guides! Inspired by Touropia, expedia, Wolters World, Beautiful Destinations, MojoTravels & Tourradar.

videos.transcript: feel the heart of Poland beating in every corner of Warsaw or varsava from its poignant memorials to its vibrant cultural scene let the city's resilient history and spirited atmosphere touch your soul here are the best things to do in Warsaw Poland number 14 Warsaw Old Town Market Square go on a journey of Discovery as you step into warsaw's Old Town Market Square this sea endless blend of past opulence and present-day Allure will leave you Enchanted from the moment you arrive located in the heart of Old Town this bustling epicenter is lined with colorful tenement houses that create a picture-perfect setting for your adventures wander through the Charming streets and absorb the vibrant atmosphere indult in a delightful array of experiences as you explore cozy cafes Boutique shops and exquisite restaurants offering a culinary Paradise one striking feature is how meticulously warsaw's glorious past has been preserved thanks to the historic reconstruction which led to the rebirth of Townhouses City walls and even the Majestic royal castle number 13. Roar saw Uprising Museum and Monument delve into the history of World War II at the Warsaw Uprising Museum this deeply moving tribute Echoes The Bravery of Warsaw residents who valiantly fought and lost their lives for an independent Poland during the 1944 uprising against German occupation this place also offers intricate narratives that reveal the true Spirit of the Polish resistance drawing you into a profound connection with the past as you explore be prepared to be moved by the poignant history depicted at the Warsaw Uprising Monument it stands not only as a symbol of significant Jewish Uprising during the Holocaust but also commemorates the unwavering quest for freedom number 12. Poland Museum of the history of Polish Jews learn about the history of Polish Jews at the pollen Museum one of Europe's Most respected museums this place offers a comprehensive narrative spanning a millennium of Jewish life in the country the Museum's dedication to education is evident through its frequent educational programs and captivating temporary exhibitions providing you with a unique and enriching experience and right in front of the museum stands a powerful symbol of remembrance the monument dedicated to the Warsaw Ghetto Heroes adorned with fresh flowers and graced by International delegations it stands as a poignant reminder of the Valor displayed during the tragic ghetto uprising of 1943. number 11. Frederick Chopin Museum if you're a fan of classical music head over to the Chopin Museum to enjoy his genre of music and pay homage to one of Poland's greatest composers housed in the Majestic ostrovsky Palace this multimedia biographical Museum boasts the largest collection of Chopin memorabilia with over 7 500 items linked to his life and work it's a rare chance to get up close and personal with the legacy of this musical genius but the real treat awaits you at the numerous Chopin piano concerts held daily in Warsaw the city takes immense pride in its musical Heritage and attending a concert performed by different talented pianists will transport you back in time to the era of Chopin himself number 10 legendki Park and Palace have a fun-filled experience at legendki Park and Palace a perfect destination for nature and architecture enthusiasts This Magnificent Park also known as the Royal baths park off offers a glimpse into the lap of luxury with its impressive architecture and breathtakingly beautiful gardens but Genki Park boasts an atmosphere of Serenity and elegance providing you with a true escape from the hustle and bustle of everyday life the appeal of this park is undoubtedly the LA Genki Palace standing proudly on a man-made Island amidst picturesque Lakes one of the favored residents of Polish monarchs the palace exudes Grandeur and opulence as you stroll through the park taking the mesmerizing sites and explore other nearby attractions like the mesh levisky Palace the Museum of hunting and horsemanship and the white Pavilion number nine Bell Tower of Saint Anne's Church the bell tower of Saint Anne's Church is perfect for anyone seeking an awesome view of warsaw's Old Town the observation deck accessible after climbing 147 steps offers panoramic views that will truly take your breath away from this vantage point you can indulge in the beauty of warsaw's historical sites and Architectural gems the church itself is a prime example of Gothic architecture with its intricate details and soaring spiers that reach Towards the Sky the bell tower a prominent feature of the church adds to its charm and Allure giving visitors a sense of awe and reverence number eight modlin Fortress located about 18 miles Northwest of Warsaw The Moth Lane Fortress is a historical site worth exploring this impressive military fortification stands at the meeting point of two rivers offering astonishing views of the surrounding Landscapes you can Unleash Your Inner Explorer by traversing the 4 fortresses nooks and crannies and marveling at its outstanding architecture lose yourself in its Maze of walls and Chambers each with its own story waiting to be discovered the fortress's surroundings also provide ample opportunities for outdoor activities from relaxed strolls along the Riverbanks to invigorating hikes through nearby Woods the area is a playground for nature enthusiasts by the way check the links in the description for recommended tour operators that will enhance your Warsaw experience and save you valuable time and effort number 7. St John's Arch cathedral St John's Arch Cathedral effortlessly Blends stunning Roman Catholic architecture with historical significance the cathedral's impressive brick Gothic design is a sight to behold captivating visitors as they stroll along the old town it's Grandeur and importance as one of the country's most sacral buildings leaves a lasting impression on all who visit inside the cathedral you'll discover a sense of tranquility and spirituality making it an ideal spot for a quiet reflection and appreciation of the Divine while you're there take your time to explore every corner gaze at the intricate details and soak in the atmosphere don't forget to attend a mass or service and the spiritual Ambience has drawn countless worshipers throughout the centuries number six karakov suburb for a relaxed stroll you've got to check out Krakow of suburb it's part of the city's famous Royal route and offers an incredible experience that will capture your attention picture yourself strolling this vibrant Street lined with beautifully rebuilt palaces lose yourself in the artistic Allure of numerous Galleries and discover Hidden Treasures in the unique shops that line the Avenue if you're a foodie you will be surprised by the gastronomic Wonders awaiting at local restaurants and Charming cafes from delectable traditional dishes to International flavors The Culinary options are sure to satiate any palette what's more this bustling Street caters to budget conscious visitors with an array of affordable and even free activities to indulge in number five Warsaw royal castle to learn more about the eventful past of Poland head to the Warsaw royal castle and take in the Regal Splendor it offers once a defensive fortification along the viswa river it now stands as a proud State Museum and a National Historical gym step inside to discover engaging exhibits that unravel the rich history of Poland admire the resilience of this iconic Castle reconstructed after World War II moreover the Castle's Vantage Point offers adorable panoramic views of the city as you gaze upon the cityscape from this UNESCO world heritage site you'll be left with an indelible memory of warsaw's beauty number four Palace of culture and science located in the modern part of the city the Palace of culture and science offers a captivating experience that will leave you in awe Ascend to new heights and witness the breathtaking view of Warsaw by taking the elevator to the 30th floor of this iconic architectural Wonder soaring at 1 230 feet above ground level it proudly stands as the second tallest building in both Warsaw and Poland the Palace of culture and science has transformed into a vibrant Hub of entertainment and excitement you can participate in diverse cultural events hosted within the palace's walls attend captivating performances art exhibitions and concerts immersing yourself in the vibrant art scene number three villanov Palace visit villanov Palace and soak up the opulent beauty of this Baroque Masterpiece the Magnificent architecture and luxurious Interiors offer visitors a glimpse into Poland's Royal past it was built in the late 17th century as a summer residence for King John III a renowned Monarch who led Poland to Victory against the Ottoman Empire in the Battle of Vienna wander through the palace's Fine Art Museum and admire its Grand Royal Apartments transporting yourself back in time as you explore the palace grounds take a moment to appreciate the meticulously manicured Gardens that surround the palace providing a Serene and picturesque setting for a leisurely stroll and to make the most of your visit consider taking a guided tour number two Warsaw University Library rooftop garden the rooftop garden atop Warsaw University Library is highly recommended for anyone seeking a Serene retreat in the heart of Warsaw as one of Europe's largest rooftop gardens this Hidden Gem offers unparalleled panoramic views of lower Warsaw showcasing The Majestic Vistula River in all its Glory you will be enthralled by the tranquility and beauty of this Oasis which has been split into two distinct sections the upper garden and the Lower Garden to fully enjoy the experience plan your visit between April and October when the upper Garden becomes public the picturesque setting is adorned with ponds a soothing Fountain and elegant Granite sculptures adding an artistic Flair to the already mesmerizing surroundings number one Copernicus Science Center embark on an educational Adventure at the Copernicus Science Center a museum dedicated to science nestled along the bank of the Vistula River this science Haven offers a thrilling Escape for curious minds and enthusiasts alike delve into the wonders of nature through interactive exhibits and immersive workshops that are sure to engage and Amaze you Unleash Your Inner scientist as you conduct Hands-On experiments and even experience the thrill of a simulated earthquake the excitement continues with the multimedia planetarium where you can explore the vastness of the cosmos outside Venture into the open air Discovery part where a world of outdoor experiments awaits challenging you to think critically and creatively click here for more things to do in Poland

videos.title: How To Spend 3 Days In WARSAW Poland | Travel Guide & Tips!
videos.description:
Ready to explore Warsaw, Poland like never before? In this video, I’m sharing the **perfect 3-day itinerary** packed with must-see landmarks, hidden gems, and local favorites that will make your trip unforgettable!  

Warsaw is a city of rich history, stunning architecture, and vibrant culture—from its charming Old Town to modern city vibes, there’s something for everyone! I’ll take you through **iconic sights, the best local eats, and insider tips** so you can experience Warsaw stress-free and like a pro.  

✨ What you’ll discover in this video:
✔️ The must-visit landmarks and their fascinating history  
✔️ Hidden gems & local hotspots most tourists miss  
✔️ The best Polish food spots (Pierogi lovers, you’re in for a treat!)  
✔️ Essential travel tips—best times to visit, local hacks & budget tips  
✔️ And much more to help you maximize your trip!  

So, if you’re dreaming of **strolling through Warsaw’s colorful streets, diving into its history, and tasting amazing Polish cuisine**, hit play and let’s plan your adventure together! 🚀  

💬 Drop a comment! What’s the first place you’d visit in Warsaw? Or share your best Warsaw travel tips below! ⬇️

💲*To streamline your travel planning, we've converted all local currency prices into USD. This simple step means easy budgeting for our international travelers, ensuring a smooth and enjoyable trip without the fuss of currency calculations!*

videos.transcript: get ready for an unforgettable 3-day Adventure in Warsaw from walking along the historic Royal route and exploring the colorful Old Town to uncovering Hidden gems in the vibrant praga District this itinerary is packed with iconic landmarks cultural experiences and local [Music] Secrets stick around until the end for Insider tips to help make the most of your Warsaw trip [Music] [Music] best time to visit Warsaw the best time to visit Warsaw is between May and September when the weather is warm and the city is bustling with outdoor events and festivals during these months temperatures range from 65 to 80° F 18 to 27° C making it ideal for exploring the city's Parks historical sites and Riverfront promenades spring and early summer offer beautiful blooming Gardens while Autumn brings a crisp atmosphere and vibrant foliage winter although cold with temperatures often below freezing below 0° C can be magical due to festive Christmas markets and holiday decorations if you don't mind the cold visiting in December offers a unique experience of warsaw's winter charm Warsaw airport tips if you're flying into Warsaw shopan airport getting to the city center is easy and affordable the skm train line S2 or S3 connects the airport to the city in about 20 minutes with tickets costing only 1 USD alternatively buses such as the 175 or the n32 Night Bus operate frequently and cost about the same taxis from the airport cost around 15 USD but it's always best to use a reputable service or an app like uber to avoid overcharging where to stay in Warsaw for a prime location close to most major attractions stay in the historic Oldtown stady miasto area here you'll find Charming boutique hotels and apartments with views of medieval architecture with prices ranging from 100 to 150 USD per night for a more contemporary Vibe check out shrewd MCH warsaw's downtown district where you can find luxury hotels such as the Intercontinental or Marriot for around 200 USD per night or budget friendly stays starting at 70 USB per night how to get around Warsaw Warsaw boasts a modern and efficient transportation system that makes navigating the city a breeze with options including buses trams and the Metro you'll never find yourself stranded a single Journey ticket costs around 1 USD while a 24-hour travel pass is available for just 5 USD offering unlimited rides on all modes of public transport for a more eco-friendly and adventurous option consider renting a bike through the city's ventor ELO bike sharing system for about 6 USD per day taxis are also readily available but apps like bolt and Uber are more affordable with rides averaging 5 to 10 USD depending on the distance day one iconic landmarks of Warsaw breakfast at Charlotte manora start your day with a delicious breakfast at Charlotte monora a trendy Cafe known for its freshly baked bread and Parisian inspired menu opt for their breakfast set which includes croissants jams and scrambled eggs for about 15 USD pair it with a latte for about 4 USD the Cozy atmosphere and Lively crowd make it the perfect spot to Kickstart your Warsaw Adventure Insider tip try their housemade chocolate spread it's a local favorite and pairs perfectly with fresh bread early morning discover warsaw's Royal begin your Warsaw Journey with a walk along the iconic Royal route starting at Castle Square in the old town Marvel at the stunning royal castle a UNESCO world heritage site entry costs 8 USD and a guided tour offers fascinating insights into the city's Royal history don't miss zigas Moon's column a symbol of warsaw's resilience and pride stroll down Kowski pred misch Street lined with historic churches monuments and upscale boutiques this picturesque Boulevard leads you through the heart of warsaw's cultural heritage Insider tip arrive early to capture the best photos without the crowds the Morning Light creates a magical glow on the Castle's facade morning visit the presidential Palace and Holy Cross church continue along Kowski pra to the presidential Palace an architectural Gem and an important political site in Poland though you can't go and side the exterior is impressive and makes for great photos just a short walk away is the Holy Cross church where the heart of famous composer Frederick chopon is interred take a moment to admire the church's broke design and reflect on Chon's Legacy Insider tip look for the small plaque outside Holy Cross church that marks where Shop's heart is kept it's a fascinating piece of History lunch at speia red gonal make your way to spe Regal a restaurant celebrated for its traditional polish Cuisine try the perogi filled with meat or spinach and cheese accompanied by a refreshing beetroot soup all for around 20 USD the rustic decor and friendly staff enhan the dining experience Insider tip don't miss their homemade compot a traditional polish fruit drink for a sweet and refreshing compliment to your meal afternoon explore warsaw's Oldtown after lunch dive into the charm of warsaw's Oldtown a beautifully reconstructed area that was destroyed during World War II begin at Market square surrounded by colorful tow houses visit St John's AR Cathedral one of the oldest churches in Warsaw and admire its Gothic architecture entry is free but donations are appreciated wander through the cobblestone streets and take in the vibrant atmosphere street performers artists and Quint cafes line the square making it a delightful place to spend the afternoon Insider tip visit the small Historical Museum located in one of the tow houses to learn about the meticulous reconstruction efforts after the war discover the Warsaw barbin next head to the Warsaw barbin a remnant of the city's medieval defensive walls walk along the cobblestone streets and enjoy the street art and performers that bring this historic area to life this is a great spot to pick up souvenirs from local vendors take a moment to admire the surrounding architecture which blends Gothic and Renaissance Styles the barbin also connects the old town to the new town offering a seamless transition into more modern parts of the city Insider tip look for the small Alleyways that lead to Hidden Courtyards and quaint cafes late afternoon multimedia Fountain Park conclude your afternoon at multimedia Fountain Park located near the Oldtown during the summer months you can enjoy spectacular evening Fountain shows set to music and lights the park is free to enter making it a perfect budget friendly attraction Insider tip arrive a bit early to grab a good viewing spot bring a light jacket as it can get chilly by the river in the evening dinner at resta for dinner head to resta a classic Polish restaurant known for its hearty dishes and oldw World charm treat yourself to their signature roasted duck with apples priced around 30 USD the warm Ambiance and traditional Decor make it an ideal place to end your first day in Warsaw the restaurant also offers an open kitchen allowing diners to watch as chefs prepare traditional polish dishes with finesse Insider tip make a reservation in advance to secure a table as especially on weekends evening stroll along the Vistula River boulevards after dinner take a leisurely stroll along the Vistula River boulevards the beautifully lit prominade offers stunning views of the city skyline and is a popular spot for both locals and tourists end your night with a drink at one of the Riverside bars where cocktails cost around 10 USD during the summer you might find popup markets and live music performances along the boulevard adding to the Lively atmosphere Insider tip look for the floating bars on the river for a unique nightlife Experience day two more famous landmarks and cultural hotpots breakfast at Meen xami Cafe kick off your second day with breakfast at Meen xami Cafe a Coy spot known for its creative menu and artsy Vibe try their signature omelet with smoked salmon or a stack of fluffy pancakes with fresh berries breakfast here costs around 12 USD and their freshly squeezed orange juice is a must try for an energizing start Insider tip this Cafe often hosts art exhibitions so take a moment to admire the local artwork on display early morning visit the Poland Museum of the history of Polish Jews after breakfast head to the Poland Museum a world class since institution that tells the 1,000-year history of Polish Jews through interactive exhibits and personal stories you'll gain a deep understanding of warsaw's Rich Multicultural past admission is about 10 USD and you can opt for an audio guide for an additional 5 USD Insider tip allocate at least 2 hours for your visit as the museum offers a vast array of exhibits that deserve your attention morning stroll through Saxon Garden a short short walk from the museum takes you to Saxon Garden one of warsaw's oldest public parks take a leisurely stroll through its manicured Lawns and admire the beautiful Baroque statues and fountains the park is a peaceful Retreat from the bustling City and offers plenty of photo opportunities Insider tip don't miss the Tomb of the Unknown Soldier located at the edge of the park witness the solemn Changing of the Guard ceremony which happens every hour lunch at Hal iiki for lunch head to halak koshiki a trendy Food Hall offering a variety of Cuisines Under One Roof whether you crave polish Delicacies Italian pasta or Asian fusion you'll find something to satisfy your pet expect to spend around 20 USD for a meal and drink Insider tip grab a seat on the Terrace if the weather is nice and enjoy the vibrant atmosphere afternoon Palace of culture and science no visit to Warsaw is complete without seeing the Palace of culture and science the tallest building in Poland and a symbol of the city take the elevator to the observation deck on the 30th floor for panoramic views of Warsaw the entry fee is about 7 USD Insider tip on clear days you can see as far as the Vistula River and Beyond Sunset is an especially beautiful time to visit explores zat trasi Mall next head to Z trasi a modern Shopping Mall located near the Palace of culture and science its distinctive glass doed roof and wide selection of shops makes it a great place for some retail therapy even if you're not in the mood to shop the architecture is worth admiring Insider tip check out the rooftop Terrace for a unique view of the surrounding skyscrapers late afternoon walk through Novi shvat Street take leisurely walk along Novi shvat Street one of warsaw's most famous boulevards lined with Cafe boutiques and historic buildings this street offers a Charming blend of the old and new stop by a local cafe for a coffee break and enjoy the Lively Ambiance dinner at alvino end your day with dinner at alvino a modern restaurant known for its innovative polish Cuisine and extensive wine collection try their seasonal tasting menu for about 50 us SD which offers a delightful culinary Journey Insider tip the staff is highly knowledgeable about wine pairings so don't hesitate to ask for recommendations evening night cap at bar studio conclude your second day with a night cap at bar Studio A hip-hop bar located in the Palace of culture and science The Eclectic decor and laid-back Vibe make it a great spot to unwind cocktails here cost around 8 USD Insider tip if you're visiting on a weekend you might catch a live music performance or a DJ set day three hidden gems and local secrets of Warsaw breakfast at Secret Life cafe begin your third day with breakfast at Secret Life cafe a Charming little spot tucked away in one of warsaw's quieter neighborhoods known for its cozy Ambiance and art Aral coffee this Cafe offers delicious breakfast options like poached eggs on sourdough toast or homemade granola with Greek yogurt expect to spend around 12 USD Insider tip grab a seat by the window for a perfect view of the quaint Street outside and start your day with some local people watching early morning explore the neon Museum dive into warsaw's more eclectic Side by visiting the neon Museum a unique attraction dedicated to preserving neon signs from the Cold War era the museum offers a nostalgic glimpse into the city's past with vibrant neon art pieces that once lit up Warsaw streets admission costs about 5 USD Insider tip check out the small gift shop for quirky souvenirs like neon themed postcards and prints morning visit praga District next head to praga a trendy District located on the Eastern Bank of the Vistula River once considered gritty praga has undergone a transformation and is now known for its artistic Vibe and hipster cafes wander through the streets and admire the street art and murals that Adorn the buildings stopped by Soho Factory a creative space housing galleries design shops and restaurants Insider tip don't miss the kesed vodka distillery where you can learn about Poland's vodka making traditions and even enjoy a tasting session lunch at PE FL gon for lunch try something truly local at PE flaki gorona a casual Eatery specializing in traditional Warsaw comfort food order a plate of py Polish potato dumplings or flocky trip soup for an authentic Taste of warsaw's culinary Heritage a hearty meal here costs about 15 USD Insider tip portions are generous so come hungry afternoon walk along Scot aevi Park after lunch take a leisurely walk through Scot aevi Park one of warsaw's most beautiful green spaces with picturesque Lakes shaded paths and sculptures scattered throughout the park it's the perfect place to relax and unwind in the spring and summer you might even spot locals rowing boats on the lake Insider tip bring a picnic blanket and some snacks if you want to enjoy a peaceful Break by the water visit the Museum of Life under communism continue your afternoon with a visit to the Museum of Life under communism an intriguing Museum that recreates everyday life in Poland during the Communist era from vintage furniture to propaganda posters the exhibits offer a fascinating glimpse into the past admission costs about 6 USD Insider tip opt for the guided tour if available it provides deeper context and interesting anecdotes about life during that time late afternoon chill at plova beach head back towards the Vistula River and unwind a plova beach a popular Urban beach with a laid-back atmosphere grab a drink from one of the beach bars kick back on a deck chair and enjoy the view of the river and Warsaw Skyline Insider tip if you're visiting in the summer you might catch a live music performance or an open movie screening dinner at Bosa praga for your final dinner in Warsaw head to Bosa praga an elegant restaurant conveniently located near plova beach known for its excellent service and delightful dishes Bosa praga offers a blend of traditional polish flavors with a modern twist try their slow cooked pork belly or the vegetarian noi with truffle sauce both priced around 25 USD the warm Ambiance and creative plating make it get an excellent choice to wrap up your Warsaw Adventure Insider tip pair your meal with a glass of Polish craft beer or their house recommended wine for a complete dining experience evening sunset views from patski bridge end your 3-day journey in Warsaw with a sunset walk along the patski bridge the view of the city skyline and the vula river bathed in Golden Light is simply breathtaking it's the perfect way to reflect on your time in this vibrant City that wraps up your 3-day Adventure in Warsaw from Iconic landmarks to Hidden gems this vibrant city has so much to offer if you enjoyed this guide check out our other videos for More Travel inspiration and don't forget to subscribe for more exciting itineraries tips and guides for your next trip

videos.title: WARSAW - Discover The Best Things To Do In Poland's Capital - Travel Guide Warsaw
videos.description:
This is the Warsaw Travel Guide with the best attractions in the capital of Poland. Discover the enchanting Old Town with the Royal Castle, walk along the Royal Road with Chopin's heart to the best viewpoint, museums and everything in between. Don't miss any attraction when visiting Warsaw.

Things to do in Warsaw  - Travel Guide Warsaw :
0:00​​ - Intro Warsaw, Poland
0:29 - Travel to City from the airport
1:01 - Warsaw Old Town
1:44 - Old Town Market Square
2:04 - Royal Road and Castle Square
2:22 - Royal Castle
2:54 - St. Anne's Church
3:11 - Presidential Palace
3:32 - Piłsudski Square and Tomb Unknown Soldier
4:04 - Chopin's Music Benches
4:17 - Copernicus statue
4:27 - Church of the holy cross (Chopin's heart)
4:53 - Park Łazienki
5:24 - Palace of Culture and Science
6:11 - Uprising Museum
6:37 - University roofgarden
6:56 - Polin Museum
7:26 - Wilanów Palace 
7:43 - Best time to visit Warsaw (weather)

Click here to subscribe: 
https://www.youtube.com/martijnaroundtheworld?sub_confirmation=1

Tickets:
Palace of Culture and Science: https://pkin.pl/en/news-and-events/palace-of-culture-and-science-guided-tours/
Royal Castle Warsaw: https://www.zamek-krolewski.pl/en/opening-hours-and-ticket-prices

Copenhagen : https://youtu.be/6TY2zE26WsY

Keywords:
#warsaw  #warsawpoland #travelwarsaw
videos.transcript: Hi, I am Martijn your guide in Warsaw. Poland's vibrant capital where history and modernity collides. I show you the best things to do in Warsaw during my 3 days visit. Explore the enchanting Old Town rebuilt in perfection after World War II... ...stroll through the Majestic royal Castle... ...pay homage to Chopin's heart at the Holy Cross church... ...soak in breathtaking views from the Palace of culture and Science... ...and wander through the lively streets filled with art and culture. The city has two airports "Modlin and Chopin" and Chopin is the biggest and is located 10 km southwest of the city... ...and named after the famous composer Frederick Chopin and in a moment more about him. To travel to the city you can use the bus service the journey takes around 40 minutes and a ticket costs around €2. The train is 10 minutes faster and will set you back around €3... ...and of course there's also a taxi service available the journey takes around 20 minutes depending on the traffic... ...and a ticket will set you back around €15. Let's start the Warsaw travel guide in the old city center... ...and locals call it "Stare Miasto". It dates back to the 13th century when it began as a small fortified settlement. Over the centuries it grew into a bustling Hub of trade and culture... ...surrounded by protective city walls. However the old town's beauty belies in its turbulent history... ...during World War II nearly 90% of the district was destroyed. What you see today is not just a charming historic center but a testament to the resilience and determination of the Polish people. Using original blueprints, photographs and paintings Warsaw painstakingly rebuilds the old town brick by brick... ...earning it a place on the Unesco's World heritage list. The old town Market Square is a must sea surrounded by cafes, restaurants and art galleries. In the center stands a bronze statue of the Warsaw mermaid. It's half a women half a fish sitting on a rock at the bank of the bay. It symbolized the strength, bravery and Independence of the city of Warsaw. And from here it's easy to explore the old city center by walking the Royal Road. It's called that way because it connects several royal buildings. A good starting point is Castle Square. It's an historic and iconic public square in town. And one of the most significant and popular spots in the city. Here you also find the Royal Castle. Once it was the residence of Polish monarchs... ...and it was heavily damaged during World War II but it has been precisely reconstructed to its former grandeur. After the war surviving fragments of the Castle like walls and foundations and sellers were saved for further destruction.... ...and in the 1970s they started the Reconstruction of this Castle. Some of the art and statues you see inside were kept hidden during the war. The next spot on the Royal Road is St. Anne's Church. It was founded in 1454 over the centuries the church has been remodeled several times... ...reflecting different architectural styles like baroque after the 17th century renovation and neoclassical in the late 18th century. I'm still on the Royal Road of so many beautiful buildings for example the presidential Palace. It holds significant historical and political importance of Poland. There was major damage to the Palace during World War II due to bombings but it was reconstructed later. Since 1994 it has served as the official residence of the president of Poland. And this is the spacious "Pilsudski Square" in the city center. Here you will see the grave of the Unknown Soldier. It's a symbolic and significant War Memorial dedicated to the unknown soldiers who have fought and died for Poland's freedom and independence throughout history. The grave was inaugurated on November the 2nd 1925... ...with the remains of an unidentified soldier brought back from a battle in 1918. In a moment you know one of the best viewing points, a peaceful Park to visit... ...and the resting place of the heart of Chopin the composer but first... ...on the Royal Road you can sit on several Stone seats just like this one... ...and every seat is equipped with music from the famous composer Frédéric Chopin and he is from Poland. Just locate the button push it and enjoy his music. And don't miss the Copernicus statue. It's in the middle of the Copernicus Square radiating out of the statue are the rings of our solar system. The Church of the Holy Cross is located next to the statue. Inside you will find the heart of the famous composer Frédéric Chopin but it's safely hidden away one of the pillars. His heart was smuggled to Warsaw by his sister following his death in Paris in 1849. Preserved in a jar of congac it was secretly brought to Poland. Although this act was illegal it fulfilled his last wish. And then some greenery the "Łazienki Park" and it's one of the most beautiful and biggest parks in the city. When you compare it to soccer fields it's around 125 fields so it's really big. One of the main attractions is the "Palace of the Isle". The palace originally began as a bathing pavilion in the late 17th century. In the 18th century the last king of Poland transformed it into a luxurious summer residence. In the park you can also find The Chopin monument. It's a statue dedicated to the famous composer. In a moment you know a great museum to visit but first the Palace of Culture and Science. Once it was a gift from of Jozef Stalin to the Polish people during the Communist period. And for a long time it was considered as the symbol of socialist power and the pride of people in Poland. Nowadays the building houses for example as cinema, theater, offices and you can book a guided tour. You can use the link in the text under the video to book your tour. And I recommend you to go to the 30th floor of the building and see the beautiful panorama of the city at a height of 114 meters. And the funny fact is that the people are over here say that the best view of the city is on the tower since you don't see him. In a moment more about one of the largest rooftop gardens in Europe which is not known by the big public... ...but first the Uprising Museum. The museum is a tribute of Warsaw's residents to those who fought and died for an independent Poland and its free capital. There are exhibits over several floors... ...containing photographs, audio and video, interactive displays, artifacts, written accounts and other testimonies... ...of how life was during the German occupation of Warsaw, the uprising and its aftermath. The city houses is a leading University and it's located in the heart of the city center. The reason I mention this is because they built a rooftop garden on the ceiling. It's a kind of Hidden Gem and you can visit it completely free of charge . That Warsaw has a turbulent history is reflected in amount of museums in the city for example the Polin Museum... ...and this museum is about a history of Polish Jews. A cultural institution which is dedicated to preserve the rich and diverse history and heritage of Polish Jews. In front of the museum is a large monument that remembers those who fought and died during the Warsaw ghetto uprising. And this is the baroque pearl of Warsaw the Wilanów Palace. A long time ago it was the summer residence of King Jan III Sobieski and his wife. I took a walk through the gardens and it was one of the most beautiful I've ever seen. What is the best time for our visit I think May, June, September or October... ...when the weather conditions are most favorable in Poland... ...and you are avoiding the high temperatures in the summer. Please give a thumbs up thank you so much and you like this Warsaw travel guide... ...and if you don't want to miss new episodes just subscribe on my YouTube channel. It's totally free and it enriches your view on the whole world.

safetyNotes: Don't miss any attraction when visiting Warsaw
And don't miss the Copernicus statue
And the funny fact is that the people are over here say that the best view of the city is on the tower since you don't see him
and you are avoiding the high temperatures in the summer
and if you don't want to miss new episodes just subscribe on my YouTube channel
